title: Chapter 1: The Beginning
content: In a quiet, moonlit night, a man stood resolute, his eyes blazing with determination as he faced a group of ten adversaries. Each wore a black coat with crimson accents, their faces hidden behind hoods, except for the leader, who donned a sinister black coat and a red and white mask. At the man’s feet lay a small baby and a sleek, black sword with an ominous aura.
The father knew he had to protect them at all costs.

With a swift motion, the father raised his sword, channeling the power of water into the blade. The air crackled with energy as he unleashed a flurry of slashes, each strike accompanied by a cascade of water that shimmered in the moonlight. Four of the attackers were overwhelmed, their bodies crumpling to the ground as the water cut through them like a tidal wave.

Seeing the danger, the father quickly created a barrier of water around the baby, the sword, and a tiny, dragon-like creature that had been watching from the shadows. The barrier shimmered, reflecting the moonlight, as the father sent it soaring towards the safety of the dense forest.

But the leader of the gang was not so easily deterred. He raised his gun, a wicked grin hidden beneath his mask, and fired a shot infused with the power of wind. The bullet whistled through the air, aimed straight at the fleeing water barrier.

The father, acting on instinct, leaped into the air. His sword flashed as he sliced the bullet in half, the pieces spiraling away harmlessly. But the leader was quick, firing another shot and shouting, "Wind Element: Wind Driver!" A torrent of wind roared from the gun, a force so powerful it tore through the night like a hurricane.

The father swung his sword, desperately trying to summon a water barrier to block the attack. But the wind was too strong. It blasted through the barrier, striking the father in mid-air and sending him hurtling towards the river beside his home, where he crashed into the water with a thunderous splash.

Meanwhile, the water barrier carrying the baby, the sword, and the tiny dragon crashed into the forest, the impact softened by the dense canopy of trees. The tiny dragon, sensing the danger, wrapped its body around the baby, its scales glowing with dark energy as it absorbed the sword, melding its power with its own to protect the child.

But the danger was far from over. Two of the gang members had followed, their eyes gleaming with malice as they approached the baby step by step. The dragon, though weakened, refused to let harm come to the child. It lashed out at the first attacker, sinking its teeth into the man's leg and releasing a burst of dark energy. The man staggered back, shocked by the unfamiliar power.

But the dragon’s strength was waning. The second attacker aimed his gun, unleashing a basic wind attack called "Wind Element: Wind Burst." The force of the blast sent the dragon flying away from the baby, crashing into the ground. The dragon struggled to stay conscious, its vision blurring as it watched the two attackers close in on the helpless child.

With each blink, the dragon’s resolve grew. It knew it had to protect the baby, no matter the cost. As the attackers drew closer, the dragon summoned every ounce of strength it had left, ready to fight to the bitter end.

title: Chapter 2: Kozo Zentos
content: As the two men approached the baby, the tiny dragon lay on the ground, struggling to keep its eyes open. The creature was on the brink of unconsciousness, its strength waning with each passing second. Just as the dragon was about to give in, a single tear fell from its eye, hitting the ground with a soft splash.

In the next moment, the earth trembled, and spikes of rock erupted from the ground, sending the two men flying away from the baby. The dragon, barely conscious, heard a calm, reassuring voice, "Don't worry, pal, I’ll take it from here." A gentle hand stroked the dragon's head, and as it looked up, it saw a man smiling down at it. He was dressed in black pants and a white shirt, neatly tucked in. With a confident thumbs-up, he assured the dragon before it finally succumbed to exhaustion and closed its eyes.

The man turned his attention to the two fallen warriors. "Come on now," he said, walking towards them with a relaxed gait. "This isn’t the way to treat a baby and a cute little creature like that. I’ll teach you a lesson."

One of the men, trying to mask his fear, warned, "Don’t mess with us, or else..." But before he could finish, he noticed that the stranger was no longer in front of him. Confused, he looked around only to find his partner lying on the ground, defeated. The man in the white shirt was casually sitting on top of him, smiling and waving as if greeting an old friend.

In a flash, the man in white was beside the remaining warrior, placing a firm hand on his shoulder. "Don’t worry," he said with a grin, "I’ll show you what happened to your friend." The air grew thick with tension as an overwhelming aura filled the space. The man whispered, "Earth Style: Ground Strike," and with a swift motion, he pushed the warrior towards the ground. The earth below responded to his command, rising up and slamming into the man’s back, sending shockwaves through the area. The force was so intense that the ground beneath them shattered, uprooting nearby trees.

With the two men defeated, the stranger walked over to the baby and the unconscious dragon. "Let’s get you somewhere safe," he said softly, glancing at the baby and then at the dragon. Using his earth elemental powers, he crafted a stone box from the ground, gently placing the baby and the tiny creature inside. Hoisting the box onto his shoulder, he carried them away from the battle scene.

Meanwhile, the rest of the group, led by their masked boss, searched the forest for the baby and the sword. When they stumbled upon their two fallen comrades, the boss, his white mask hiding his expression, asked in a menacing tone, "What happened here?"
One of the beaten men, still dazed, muttered, "I don’t remember anything..."

The other, trembling, suddenly recalled the last words the man in white had said before leaving. With a trembling voice, he stammered, "H-his name... his name is Ko... Kozo Zen... Zentos." Summoning all his courage, he managed to say it clearly, "Kozo Zentos."

title: Chapter 3: Shiron and Nichuki
content: After 15 years...

In a dense forest, the sound of chirping birds and the gentle rustle of leaves created a calming atmosphere. A boy stood at the top of a tall tree, feeling the wind on his face. He wore black shoes, a red T-shirt, and a blue jacket, his long black hair tied in a tail. His sharp, captivating black eyes gleamed as he looked out across the horizon. "It's a nice day to explore," he said, smiling as the wind played with his hair.

With a swift movement, he leapt from one tree to another, wind element gathering at his legs, propelling him forward. He dashed from tree to tree until he reached the top of a towering mountain. At the edge of the cliff, he shouted, "Brenton!" and jumped.

A large black dragon with two sharp fangs burst through the sky, its speed unmatched. The boy landed gracefully on the dragon’s back, laughing. "Let’s go! Let's go!" he exclaimed with excitement, flying over the water and soaring towards the clouds, his heart racing with joy.

As they glided through the air, the boy noticed they had ventured far from home. Below them, a vast city with towering walls and bustling people stretched out. "Brenton, let's go down and check it out!" he suggested. The dragon shook its head, clearly reluctant. Following Kozo’s orders, Brenton was unwilling to land. The boy grinned mischievously. "Come on, Kozo will never know! Let’s have some fun."

Without warning, the boy jumped off Brenton's back, landing in the city below. He gazed in awe at the tall buildings and lively streets. Despite Brenton’s reluctance, the dragon followed him down, unwilling to let the boy roam alone.

As they wandered through the city, people stopped in their tracks, eyes wide in shock. Dragons were not a common sight here, and Brenton’s presence drew quite a crowd. Noticing the growing attention, the boy muttered, "Uh oh, I think we should leave, Brenton."

Before they could make their escape, two city scouts armed with spears approached. They aimed at the dragon, intent on containing the creature. "Wait! Don't harm him!" the boy pleaded. "He won’t do anything!"

The scouts ignored his words and moved in to attack. As one of them thrust his spear towards Brenton, the boy leaped forward. "Wind element: Wind Away!" he shouted, sending the scouts flying into the nearby walls with a gust of wind. "Oops, I overdid it... Sorry, you two."
One scout, dazed, muttered to his companion, "He’s a wind element user... We need to inform headquarters."

Realizing the situation was escalating, the boy turned to Brenton. "I think it's time to go!" he exclaimed, hopping back onto the dragon's back. They soared into the sky, but before they could leave, a voice rang out.

"Wait! Take me with you!" A girl, around the boy’s age, jumped from the rooftop of a nearby building. She had long black hair, a red top, and blue pants. She landed on Brenton, wrapping her arms tightly around the boy, causing him to choke. "Hey, I can’t breathe!" he gasped.

The girl giggled and loosened her grip, flashing a bright smile. Her blue eyes sparkled, and the boy’s heart began to race as if he knew her from long ago. She seemed familiar, like someone he was destined to protect. Snapping out of his thoughts, the boy pushed her away. "It’s not polite to jump on others like that! You’re not welcome here—get down!"
The girl playfully pushed his face away with her hand. "I don’t want to go back. Let me come with you!" she pleaded.

As they bickered like children, several men in brown scout uniforms appeared, tracking the girl’s movements. They began firing bullets imbued with fire elements at the dragon, but Brenton skillfully dodged each attack.
Alert, the boy asked the girl, "Who are those people? Why are they attacking you?"
Feigning innocence, the girl pouted, but the boy wasn’t convinced. "Stop with that face and answer me!" he demanded.

Before she could respond, a barrage of fireball bullets rained down on them. Brenton dodged most of them, but one hit him in the fang, sending them spiraling down into the forest below. Brenton crashed to the ground, creating a massive impact. The boy and girl landed safely on the trees, jumping down to the forest floor.
They were quickly surrounded by eight men armed with guns. "You’ve got nowhere to run," one of the men said.
The boy, feigning innocence, pointed to the girl. "I don’t even know her! You can take her and let us go."
The girl gasped dramatically. "Shame on you! That’s no way to treat a lady! By the way, what’s your name?" she asked with a teasing grin.

Before the boy could answer, one of the men reached out to grab the girl by her hair. She swiftly countered with a flying kick, using a small amount of water element, knocking him out cold. Two more men charged at her, shouting, "Fire element: Fire Burst!" and launched huge fireballs.

The boy was about to intervene but stopped when he saw the girl smiling confidently. She pulled two small daggers from her back and countered the fire attacks with water slashes. "Water element: Water Slashes!" she called, slicing through the flames. But just as she began to gain the upper hand, one of the men shot her from behind with a fire-imbued bullet. She fell to the ground, surrounded by the remaining attackers.

Looking up at the boy, her eyes filled with fear. His heart pounded loudly, and everything around him seemed to slow down. As one of the men prepared to strike her, she closed her eyes, bracing for impact. But when she opened them, the boy was standing in front of her, the attacker lying unconscious at his feet.

He had moved with incredible speed, wind element in his legs, delivering a powerful blow that shook the ground. The remaining four men, seeing his strength, backed away and fired more fire bursts at the pair. The girl shouted, "Get down!" but the boy laughed.

"Get behind me," he said calmly. "Water element: Water Tornado!" He struck the ground, creating a vortex of water that swallowed the fireballs. In the next moment, he dashed forward, knocking out each man with swift, precise strikes.
The girl, shocked by his abilities, stared at him in awe. The boy extended his hand. "I’m Shiron Vantes. Who are these people, and why are they after you?"
With a big smile, the girl replied, "I’m Nichuki Kentotha. I’ll explain everything, but first, we need to get out of here."
Shiron glanced at the sky. "Oh no, I’m late! I need to go back!" He called out, "Brenton, let’s move!"
Hopping onto Brenton, Shiron turned to Nichuki and offered his hand. "Come on, get on."
Together, they flew off into the sky, heading back to Shiron’s home.

title: Chapter 4: Sword and Locket
content: Shiron and Nichuki were flying back to Kozo, with Shiron asking her repeatedly, 'Who are you?' but Nichuki, with a playful innocence, just kept smiling without answering.

As they finally reached home, Shiron cautiously approached the house, stepping carefully, one foot at a time. 'Follow me, and don’t make any sound,' he whispered to Nichuki. She nodded but walked normally, deliberately stepping on a stick that snapped loudly. Shiron turned around, his face full of frustration, while Nichuki put on an innocent face as if it wasn’t intentional, but inside, she was laughing.
With a pained and pleading look, Shiron asked, 'Can you please not make any sound?'

Suddenly, a loud voice boomed, 'Shiron! I know you didn’t do your daily routine and went wandering again!' Shiron froze, his voice breaking as he stammered, 'K-Kozo, I did do the daily routine! Ask Brenton if you don’t believe me… Right, Brenton?'
Just then, Brenton, trying to avoid any trouble, pretended to be asleep, which made Nichuki burst into laughter. Shiron panicked, 'Hey! Don’t laugh! He’ll hear us!'
Kozo emerged from the house, his voice stern. 'Hmm, hmm. And who might she be?'

Shiron, sensing trouble, slowly stepped backward, trying to sneak away. But when he turned around to run, Kozo was right in front of him. 'Shiron,' Kozo said in a long, drawn-out voice, 'Are you trying to run away? And tell me, who is she?'
Shiron whistled, pretending not to hear. 'Who? I don’t know who she is… I think Brenton brought her here,' he said, looking everywhere but at Kozo.

Kozo glanced at Brenton and said, 'Ah, I see. So that’s what happened.' He spoke as if conversing with Brenton, though Brenton said nothing. Nichuki, now curious, thought to herself, 'Seriously, can he talk to him?'

Kozo then turned to Shiron, put his hand on Shiron’s shoulder, and laughed as if he had figured everything out. Nichuki, realizing this was normal for them, thought, 'Wait, he really can talk to that creature?'

In an instant, Kozo tossed Shiron to the other side and locked him down with earth-style magic. 'I told you, Shiron! Never go wandering off without my permission! And never venture near the places where people live!' Kozo growled.
Shiron, now scared, begged, 'I swear, I won’t go anywhere without your permission!'

Suddenly, an arrow, charged with wind energy, shot toward Nichuki. Before it could strike, Kozo raised his hand and blocked it, shouting, 'You cowards! You dare attack in my presence? Show yourselves!'

Shiron, breaking free from the earth’s grip with sheer strength, stood beside Nichuki, his face now serious. 'Whoever you are, I don’t care. From now on, I will protect her, so you’d better back off!' he declared.
Kozo chuckled. 'Now, now, boy. Don’t get ahead of yourself. I’m still here, and you owe me an explanation about her.'
Suddenly, a rain of arrows filled the sky. Nichuki yelled, 'Look out! We need to take cover!'
But Shiron and Kozo just laughed. 'Don’t worry,' Shiron said confidently. 'As long as Kozo is here, they can’t touch us.'

Kozo smirked and calmly said, 'Earth Element: Earth’s Guard.' The ground rose up, blocking all the arrows, while Kozo followed up with, 'Earth Element: Earth Storm.' The earth erupted, sending sand and debris flying toward the direction from which the arrows came.
The storm destroyed many trees, exposing around 50 men, all dressed in brown. Kozo grinned. 'This is going to be fun,' he said.

Shiron shouted, 'Brenton!' In a flash of darkness, Brenton appeared in the air. Shiron leaped toward the dark flash, summoning a black sword. 'Water Element: Water Flash!' he shouted, swinging the sword down, aiming at the group of attackers.
But before he could land a blow, Kozo jumped up, pushed him back to the ground, and said, 'Now’s not the time.'

Nichuki watched in awe as Shiron wielded a sword with a dragon-shaped handle, the blade dark and mysterious. But then, something strange happened. The locket around Nichuki’s neck began to glow. She took it off, trying to see what was happening. To everyone’s surprise, the sword pulled the locket toward itself, and the block in the locket fit perfectly into the empty space in the sword’s handle. A brilliant blue light shot up into the sky, swirling the clouds above.
Kozo, noticing the change, rushed over. 'Where did you get that locket?' he asked Nichuki urgently. But Nichuki, focused on removing the block from the sword, didn’t answer.
Kozo shouted louder, 'Girl, where did you get the locket?!'
Ignoring him, Nichuki kept trying to pull the block out. Shiron, seeing the tension, grabbed the sword from her. 'Kozo is asking you a question! Answer him, you idiot!'
Nichuki, furious, screamed, 'Give me my mother’s locket back, you fool!' Her voice echoed with such emotion that Kozo, Brenton, and Shiron fell silent.

Shiron’s mind flashed back to a conversation he once had with Kozo. Kozo had told him, 'When I found you, Brenton was by your side, trying to protect you. Years later, Brenton showed me this sword and said it belonged to your father.'
Shiron, remembering this, asked, 'Kozo, where is my father now?'
Kozo sighed. 'I don’t know. When I went to the place Brenton mentioned, I found no one.'
'Do you think something bad happened to him?' Shiron asked, his voice trembling.
Kozo shook his head. 'I don’t know, kid. All I know is that your father tried to protect you and this sword from some very bad people. Let’s hope we find him someday.'
Tears welled up in Shiron’s eyes as he realized the truth. 'That’s why I felt the urge to protect her when I first saw her. She’s my sister.'
